title: cracca pusilla
intro: Cracca pusilla, also known as small bird's foot trefoil or small bird's foot clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by small, yellow flowers and trifoliate leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing symptoms of allergies, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Cracca pusilla is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
